meta.gdprClause: I consent to the processing of my personal data for the purposes necessary to carry out the recruitment process in accordance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 27 April 2016 (GDPR).

basics.name: Kamil Kacperek
basics.label: Frontend Engineer
basics.summary: Frontend Engineer with 4+ years of experience in TypeScript and React. I work with the modern frontend ecosystem (Next.js, Mantine, TanStack Query) and tools for interactive UIs (Three.js, PixiJS). I have backend experience with Node.js and NestJS, which helps me design and integrate APIs effectively. I focus on performant, well-crafted applications with an emphasis on UX, code quality, and technical clarity.

work.summary: I build and maintain interactive web applications—from foundational technologies (HTML, JS, SCSS) to advanced React and Next.js (TypeScript) with 2D and 3D visualizations. I also deliver REST APIs in NestJS in coordination with the client layer. I regularly join client meetings to refine requirements, review progress, and discuss technical options.
highlights: Design and implementation of components using React, Next.js, and TypeScript.
Development of interactive 3D (Three.js) and 2D (PixiJS) visualizations for product configurators.
Design and rollout of a company-wide admin panel template.
Development of a cross-platform mobile application using Capacitor.js (Android, iOS).
Creation and deployment of reusable Web Components.

Development of REST APIs using Node.js and NestJS, including data modeling and integration with frontend applications; designing and implementing JWT-based authentication (token issuance, validation, client-side handling; refresh aligned with the product architecture).

work.summary: Worked with patients in a clinical environment, requiring strong interpersonal skills, teamwork, and the ability to make decisions under time pressure. This experience significantly enhanced my communication and organizational skills, which I now apply when collaborating on IT projects.
highlights: Collaborated closely with other physiotherapists to plan and conduct patient therapy sessions.
Adapted communication and treatment methods to individual patient needs.
Planned and organized therapy requiring effective team coordination.

education.institution: University School of Physical Education in Wrocław
